steps.title: Calculate cost per gram of protein for each food
steps.content:
From the table, extract the cost and protein amount for each food, then divide to find cost per gram of protein.

For a large egg: $0.36 costs 6 grams of protein, so cost per gram is $$\frac{0.36}{6} = 0.06 \text{ dollars per gram}$$

For a cup of milk: $0.24 costs 8 grams of protein, so cost per gram is $$\frac{0.24}{8} = 0.03 \text{ dollars per gram}$$

steps.title: Form the ratio of cost per gram
steps.content:
The ratio of cost per gram in a large egg to cost per gram in a cup of milk is $$\frac{0.06}{0.03} = \frac{6}{3} = 2$$

That matches choice A — A is correct.
